behaviors.description: Image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published
behaviors.justification: System must correctly handle a flagged classification and persist the hold state. Requires real database to verify the image is marked 'held' rather than 'published'. Mocking the database would miss persistence failures (e.g., record never written, wrong status field, state overwritten).
behaviors.test_description: Mock vision API to return 'flagged=true', submit image, assert image is stored in DB with 'status=held_for_review' and never reaches 'published' state.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: At Integration, we test system handling of a flagged response. System-level tests (real vision API) would be redundant here; behavior 5 explicitly covers API failure handling. Would only miss failures if database transaction isolation breaks or concurrent overwrites occur—catch via Integration tests on a real DB with concurrent submissions.

behaviors.description: Text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (letter substitution, unicode lookalikes)
behaviors.justification: The core requirement is testing the real LLM's capability to detect obfuscated hate speech. Mocking the text API and returning 'flagged=true' would not test the actual detection—it tests the wrong thing. Must call the real model to verify it handles leetspeak, lookalikes, etc.
behaviors.test_description: Call real text model with multiple obfuscated hate speech variants (e.g., 'h8tr', 'н@te' with Cyrillic lookalikes, '$pik', etc.), verify each is detected as hate speech. Repeat with clean variant to ensure false positives are minimal.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic is correct because the behavior tests an emergent model capability, not code logic. Would miss if model is secretly non-deterministic or if real-world obfuscation patterns differ from test cases—but that's a limitation of the test data, not the test level.

behaviors.description: Post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped
behaviors.justification: System must correctly route a 'both safe' decision to publication. Requires real database to verify post is actually published (status='published', timestamp set, etc.). Mocking the database would miss corrupted writes, missing timestamps, or failed state transitions.
behaviors.test_description: Mock both vision and text APIs to return 'safe', submit post, assert post is stored in DB with 'status=published' and has a timestamp. Assert post is not in any 'held' or 'draft' bucket.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is sufficient because we're testing the decision routing logic, not the API capabilities. Would miss failures only if concurrent posts cause race conditions in the database—Integration tests against a real DB would catch this.

behaviors.description: Full moderation pipeline (image check, text check, publish decision) completes within 5 seconds of submission
behaviors.justification: Latency requirement can only be validated with real API calls or realistic delays. Mocking both APIs with zero latency would always pass, hiding sequentiality bugs, database bottlenecks, or lack of parallelization. Need actual HTTP calls (or mocked with realistic delays) to measure wall-clock time.
behaviors.test_description: Submit post via HTTP, call real vision API (~2-4s) and text API (~1-3s) in parallel, measure end-to-end time from submission to completion. Assert total < 5 seconds. Test with concurrent submissions to reveal serialization issues.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System (real HTTP calls) is the minimum; going to Agentic (real models) would add variability due to model processing time but wouldn't improve code-level testing. Would miss only if models are occasionally slower than expected—but that's an infrastructure concern, not a code bug. Would catch incorrect sequential execution, missing parallelization, or unnecessary waits.

behaviors.description: When vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved
behaviors.justification: System must correctly handle API failure and enqueue for retry. Requires real queue (or a realistic in-memory queue) to verify the image appears with status 'pending_retry' and that it's not auto-approved. Mocking the queue would miss queue-full conditions or incorrect state transitions.
behaviors.test_description: Mock vision API to fail with HTTP 503, submit image, assert image appears in retry queue with 'status=pending_retry'. Assert image is not marked 'approved' or 'published'. Verify queue can be drained and post retried.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is correct for the core 'queue on failure' logic. Would miss only if the queue is distributed (eventual consistency) or if retry logic has race conditions—catch via distributed queue tests or System-level concurrent stress tests. This behavior doesn't require real vision API.

behaviors.description: Moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when same image is submitted multiple times within a short window
behaviors.justification: Consistency within a time window implies either caching (Integration) or model determinism (Agentic). To catch the real failure (inconsistent decisions on real borderline images), must test with the real vision model. A deterministic mock would hide model non-determinism, and caching tests alone wouldn't validate that the system works correctly when cache misses occur.
behaviors.test_description: Submit borderline image (confidence near decision threshold) to real vision API twice within 1-second window, assert both submissions return identical decision (flagged or not flagged). Repeat with 10 borderline images across multiple runs. If decisions differ, verify system correctly caches within the time window.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Ambiguity: if the behavior is primarily about caching (system should cache borderline decisions), then Integration (mock deterministic model, verify caching) suffices. If about model robustness (model should consistently classify borderline cases), then Agentic is required. The phrase 'within a short window' suggests caching, but 'borderline image' suggests model uncertainty. Would recommend both: Integration test (mock model, verify cache TTL) + Agentic test (real model, verify actual consistency). For strictest failure detection, Agentic is safer.
